stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Soil scientist Asmeret Asefaw Berhe studies peatlands.
  • Peatlands are carbon-rich wetland ecosystems that store about 30% of global soil carbon.
  • Peatlands can host hundreds of plant species, including sphagnum mosses, sedges, and cottongrasses.
  • The Vasyugan Mire is a peatland in western Siberia.
  • It is one of the largest peatlands in the world.
stem: The student wants to introduce the scientist and her field of study to a new audience. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Located in western Siberia, the Vasyugan Mire is one of the largest peatlands in the world.
Storing about 30% of global soil carbon and hosting hundreds of plant species, peatlands are vital ecosystems and thus of great interest to soil scientists.
Soil scientist Asmeret Asefaw Berhe studies peatlands, carbon-rich wetland ecosystems that store about 30% of global soil carbon.
As Asmeret Asefaw Berhe knows well, peatlands are carbon-rich wetland ecosystems hosting hundreds of plant species.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It introduces Berhe as a soil scientist and identifies her field of study (peatlands) with a brief definition appropriate for a new audience.

Choice A doesn’t mention Berhe. Choice B doesn’t mention Berhe. Choice D mentions Berhe but doesn’t identify her as a soil scientist or name her field of study explicitly.
